topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sheer audacity of it made my jaw clench. I found the journal open on the desk, the page dog-eared to a particularly sentimental entry about a terrible first date. My breath hitched, a sharp, involuntary intake of air. I didn't speak immediately; instead, I just stared, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of my textbook. When Liam finally looked up, a faint, overly innocent smile playing on his lips, I felt a slow burn rising in my chest. "What are you doing?" The tone of my voice was dangerously level, stripped bare of any warmth. He shuffled his feet, avoiding my gaze, and the silence in the cramped room thickened, heavy with unspoken accusation.

[story 2]

I slammed the laptop shut with enough force to rattle the cheap particleboard shelving. My vision swam for a second, a hot flush creeping up my neck. There, lying innocently beside my discarded coffee mug, was my private diary, its pages clearly marked with Liam’s smudged fingerprints. I didn't yell. Instead, I walked over, my movements stiff and deliberate, and snatched the book. My hands were trembling slightly as I flipped through the pages, the handwriting—his handwriting—a physical assault on my composure. When he entered the room, looking bewildered, I simply held the journal up, my eyes locked on his. "Did you... read this?" The question came out tight, laced with a brittle edge that made him visibly shrink.

[story 3]

I paced the small perimeter of the room, the worn carpet feeling suddenly abrasive beneath my bare feet. Every nerve ending felt frayed. It wasn't the discovery itself that bothered me most; it was the casualness with which he had invaded my space, the sheer lack of respect. I stopped pacing abruptly, planting my hands on my hips, forcing myself to keep my voice low and even, though the tension radiating off me was palpable. "I noticed my journal was open," I stated, the words flat and devoid of inflection. I watched his face cycle through confusion and a dawning realization. He mumbled something incoherent, fiddling with a loose thread on his hoodie, and I just shook my head slowly, a gesture that communicated volumes about my current state of profound irritation.
